marketState: neutral-to-bearish - fairly priced near-term but capped by a storage surplus and bearish positioning
summary: Front-month NG is trading almost exactly at its calendar fair value ($2.892 vs $2.923 fair, -1% spread), and the fair-value curve itself drifts up toward ~$3.00 over the next three weeks, so price-vs-fair is a neutral-to-mild-bullish input rather than a clear signal either way. Seasonality carries a slight bearish tilt (-15.1% YTD vs a -6.9% median year, only 38% odds of higher prices in 3-4 weeks, typical 4-week move -1.4%) but reliability is flagged 'Low' this week, so it shouldn't be weighted heavily. The bigger drag is fundamentals: storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average and this week's +41 Bcf build beat the +19 Bcf forecast (a bearish surprise), while managed money remains net short (35th percentile of the past 5 years) - both consistent with a supply overhang capping rallies. Technically, every timeframe from 1h to 1w shows a bearish trend with 50<200 EMA crosses, though daily/weekly RSI (34.6/36.5) is nearing oversold, arguing against fresh selling at current levels. The one clearly bullish input is near-term weather: demand over the next 1-7 days is running +20.3% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range, though that heat pulse fades toward roughly normal by days 8-16 (-3.9%).

longTerm.entry: $2.90-$2.98 (scale in on bounces toward fair value)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk, scale in over 2 entries
longTerm.rationale: Storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise, managed money is net short and not yet extreme (35th percentile, room to add), and daily/weekly technicals show a bearish trend with 50<200 EMA crosses across every timeframe, all pointing toward the weak Jan seasonal trough over this horizon.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained close above $3.10, an 8-16D weather flip back above the 5-year normal, or a bullish storage surprise (draw or smaller-than-forecast build) would invalidate the short thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D demand is +20.3% above the 5-year norm near the top of its range, front-month is FAIR-to-slightly-cheap versus the model ($2.892 vs $2.923 fair), and the curve shows mild backwardation into Sep/Oct - all consistent with a near-term bounce toward fair value even though the broader 1h-1w trend remains bearish.
shortTerm.invalidation: A fade in the 1-7D heat pulse, a break and hold below $2.78, or renewed 1h/4h bearish momentum (fresh EMA/MACD downside) would invalidate this trade.

keyDrivers: Storage +180 Bcf above 5y avg with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise this week
Managed-money COT net short at 35th percentile of 5y range (bearish, not extreme)
Near-term (1-7D) weather demand +20.3% vs 5y near top of range, fading toward normal by 8-16D
Price at fair value ($2.892 vs $2.923) with the fair-value curve drifting up toward ~$3.00 over 21 days
Bearish trend + 50<200 EMA cross on every technical timeframe 1h-1w, but daily/weekly RSI near oversold
risks: Weather forecasts can flip quickly - 8-16D demand has already faded to -3.9% vs 5y, and a hotter revision would squeeze shorts
Seasonality reliability is flagged 'Low' this week (59pp spread), so the historical pattern may not hold
Daily/weekly RSI near oversold (34.6/36.5) raises the risk of a sharp relief rally against short positioning
